title: Stelis ferrelliae
intro: Stelis ferrelliae, also known as Ferrell's stelis, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis ferrelliae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are the most common herbal preparations made with Stelis ferrelliae.
taxonomy: Stelis ferrelliae is a species of orchid, classified within the genus Stelis, and further grouped under the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. This classification reflects its close relationship with other Stelis species. The genus Stelis is generally recognized for its small, delicate flowers, often with subtle colors. Stelis ferrelliae, like other species in the Pleurothallidinae subtribe, is known for its intricate and often tiny flowers. The Orchidaceae family, to which Stelis ferrelliae belongs, comprises one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants.
